extraction_reasoning: The litigation table has “Against our Company” rows of 1 criminal, 1 civil, 11 tax and 21 statutory/regulatory cases; “Against our Promoters” has 4 criminal and 2 statutory/regulatory cases, and “Against our Directors” has 4 criminal and 2 statutory/regulatory cases. I counted only the against rows: criminal 1+4+4 = 9, regulatory 21+2+2 = 25, and tax 11 for the company; there are no “By” rows, and promoter/director personal amounts were excluded from the aggregate. The company-only quantified amount is ₹8.09 million civil + ₹95.15 million tax + ₹56.40 million regulatory = ₹159.64 million, or ₹15.964 crore; no subsidiary row is disclosed. The contingent-liabilities table is dated September 30, 2020, uses its stated Total of ₹252.44 million, converted to ₹25.244 crore; this table total includes the disclosed capital-account commitment. No company-wide top-five or top-ten customer concentration percentage is disclosed; the 50.84% Fiscal 2020 southern-region sales figure is geographic concentration, not customer concentration, and is based on total income. Monetary tables use ₹ million, converted to crore by dividing by 10.
